t_ch: 如此一来，我和那位行商便两清了。
t_en: With this, the debt between the traveling merchant and me is clear.

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 不用在意。接着睡一会儿吧，等你醒来便会忘记这些细节。
t_en: Don't worry about it. You should sleep for a little longer. By the time you wake up, you won't remember any of these details.

t_ch: 我会委托别人送你回去…这么说来，倒是有群可靠的朋友，和你颇有渊源。
t_en: I'll arrange for someone to send you home... which reminds me, you seem to have some dependable friends with close ties to you.

t_ch: 不妨就跟着他们走吧。多听些过去的事，对康复也有好处。
t_en: You should go with them. It can only help with your recovery to hear more about your past.

t_ch: 莫非…这已不是第一次了吗？
t_en: Could it be that... this isn't the first time?

t_ch: 那是…看起来很熟悉。
t_en: That... looks so familiar.

t_ch: 她…想带我去什么地方吗？
t_en: Does she want to lead me somewhere...?

t_ch: 「不必疑惑，继续向前吧。」
t_en: "Don't question it. Keep moving forward."

t_ch: 又是您，谁在和我说话？
t_en: It's you again. Who's talking to me?

t_ch: 这…能被称为共处吗？
t_en: Is this called... coexisting?

t_ch: 「别担心，我说过你并非独自一人。」
t_en: "Don't worry. As I said before, you're not alone."

t_ch: 这条路好暗。可不知为何，越往前走，心里就越平静……
t_en: This path is so dark, yet it's strange, the farther I walk... the more at peace I feel...

t_ch: 你…不要紧吧？
t_en: Are... Are you alright?

t_ch: 我这是晕过去了？
t_en: Did I pass out?

t_ch: 一小会而已。
t_en: Only for a little while.

t_ch: 没进流梦礁，没事。
t_en: It's okay. You didn't end up in Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 刚到遗像制作环节。
t_en: We were just about to make a memorial portrait of you.

t_ch: 还真够利索的啊。
t_en: A little quick to the draw, aren't you?

t_ch: 「调律」竟反过来影响了万维克，这位女士不简单啊。
t_en: The tuning rebounded and affected Wonweek instead. This is no ordinary woman.

t_ch: 事到如今，也不必向你隐瞒。
t_en: I suppose there's no point in hiding it from you anymore.

t_ch: 停云小姐本是仙舟人，此前不幸遭遇绝灭大君，被对方窃夺了身份，濒临死境。虽然逃得生天，却身受重伤，依照常理不可能恢复。
t_en: Miss Tingyun is originally from the Xianzhou. She had an unfortunate encounter with a Lord Ravager, who robbed her of her identity and left her on the verge of death. While she miraculously escaped with her life, her body suffered terrible injuries, beyond all power to heal.

t_ch: 但在此时却出现了变量吗？
t_en: But something happened to change that outcome?

t_ch: 嗯，一位天才介入了此事。她在生命领域颇有建树，为停云小姐换得了一线生机。
t_en: Yes, a genius got involved. She's very accomplished in the field of life sciences and managed to grant Miss Tingyun a shot at survival.

t_ch: 后来，列车受她委托，护送停云小姐回乡。途中在匹诺康尼停泊，也有部分静养的意图。
t_en: After that, she asked the Express to escort Miss Tingyun back home. We made a stop at Penacony along the way, partly to allow her some time to convalesce.

t_ch: 实在抱歉，小女子本以为躲进梦里便不会波及他人，不曾想意外是一桩接着一桩。
t_en: I'm so sorry. I thought no one would be affected if I hid inside a dream. I didn't expect so many mishaps to happen.

t_ch: 波及没事，别是伤及就行。喂，能不能给我口喝的？感觉自己像是睡了十年。
t_en: That's alright, so long as nobody was hurt. Can I get something to drink? I feel like I've been asleep for ten years.

t_ch: 也好，方才的骚乱引起了不少注意。我们换个地方说话吧。
t_en: That's fine. We should find another place to talk anyway. We drew some attention with that commotion earlier.

t_ch: 不久之后——
t_en: Shortly after—

t_ch: 商业模式倒是颇有意思……
t_en: The business model is quite interesting...

t_ch: 回了仙舟，商团也寻个代言可好？
t_en: Perhaps a merchant guild representative can be found upon returning to the Xianzhou?

t_ch: 这当真是我自己支的摊子…？
t_en: Did I really make this stall?

t_ch: 这可卖给谁去？
t_en: Who can I sell this to?

t_ch: 人还当真是到了也不自知……
t_en: Didn't even truly realize that one was here...

t_ch: 若是自幼习武，莫非便能……
t_en: If you have been refined since birth, perhaps you could...

t_ch: 便是眼下「掰开揉碎」……
t_en: Take this apart piece by piece...

t_ch: 恩公为何偏偏对此情有独钟……
t_en: Why do you have such a fondness for this, benefactor...?

t_ch: 我倒还是想不明白……
t_en: I still don't understand...

t_ch: 除却那位将军或许喜欢……
t_en: Aside from the fact that the general might like it...

t_ch: 放在仙舟怕是没什么销路……
t_en: Placing this on the Xianzhou won't net any sales...

t_ch: 但若换个地方……
t_en: But somewhere else...

t_ch: 都说此物能窥人深梦……
t_en: They say this item can peer into dreams...

t_ch: 不知找错了没有？
t_en: Not sure if I have the right person or not?

t_ch: 果然，调律自身是最为棘手的事。
t_en: As expected, the trickiest thing is tuning yourself.

t_ch: 这道不协和音，实在太刺耳了。
t_en: These dissonant sounds are much too harsh.

t_ch: 旧日的回声，你们不必消去。
t_en: Echoes of the past, you need not fade away.

t_ch: 随我一同，行向群星。
t_en: Follow me, to the stars.

t_ch: 其时已至，揭晓吧……
t_en: The time has come. Let it be revealed...

t_ch: 于我而言，它是否「无疑」？
t_en: Is it "inevitable" for me?

t_ch: 又是苏乐达，我可是本地人啊……
t_en: SoulGlad again... I'm a local, you know.

t_ch: 将就一下吧。
t_en: You'll have to make do.

t_ch: 总之，感谢二位出手相助。
t_en: In any case, I must thank the both of you for your assistance.

t_ch: 只是举手之劳。既然停云女士的问题已经解决，接下来是随你们去见另两位同伴么？
t_en: It was nothing. Now that the matter with Miss Tingyun is resolved, are we meeting your other two companions next?

t_ch: 不用了。万维克先生状况不佳，我们先去完成你的告别之行。
t_en: There's no need for that. Mr. Wonweek is in poor condition, so let's wrap up your farewells first.

t_ch: 停云小姐的事就拜托你们了。
t_en: I'll leave Miss Tingyun in your hands.

t_ch: 啊？杨叔…仙舟使者就快到了，你不去吗？
t_en: Huh? But Mr. Yang, the messenger from the Xianzhou is almost here. You're not coming with us?

t_ch: 交给你们，我放心。而且姬子也在，任何拿不准的她来定夺就好。
t_en: I trust that you can handle it. Besides, Himeko will be there. She'll be able to handle any decisions should you run into any difficulties.

t_ch: 好吧。那咱们得抓紧时间啦。
t_en: Okay. We better hurry then.

t_ch: 下一站，你准备去哪？
t_en: Where are you planning to go next?

t_ch: 可否给我一些思考的时间？
t_en: Could you give me some time to think about it?

t_ch: 那我们继续走着？
t_en: Shall we keep going, then?

t_ch: 要去哪？
t_en: Where are we going?

t_ch: 我们是要干啥来着…
t_en: What are we doing here again?

t_ch: 怎么这就忘了呀！停云小姐要回联盟了，咱们是来替她挑伴手礼的。
t_en: Did you forget already? Miss Tingyun is going back to the Alliance, so we're here to help her pick out souvenirs.

t_ch: 小女子人生地不熟，担心被黑心商贾下了套，恰好送礼对象都是各位熟悉的仙舟朋友。得劳烦二位大股东多提点意见啦。
t_en: I'm not too familiar with this planet. I'm worried an unscrupulous cutthroat merchant will try to take advantage of me. Luckily, the people I want to buy these gifts for are friends you already know on the Xianzhou, so I'll be counting on both your stakeholder intuitions for recommendations!

t_ch: 哪有商人能给你下套啊……
t_en: As if a merchant could seriously take advantage of you...

t_ch: 欢迎，几位有什么需要？
t_en: Welcome! How can I help you?

t_ch: 这位老板好，小女子初来乍到，想为故乡的朋友带点梦境特产，两位本地通点名推荐了这儿，咱就来看看。
t_en: Hello there! It's my first time visiting the Dreamscape, so I wanted to buy some local specialties as souvenirs for my friends back home. My two friends here are more familiar with the Dreamscape and recommended your shop, so I'm here to take a look.

t_ch: 哎呀，客人您真会说话，来——随便瞧。
t_en: Why, you really know how to flatter! Go ahead — browse all you like.

t_ch: 第一位是太卜司的符玄大人，您看挑些什么合适？
t_en: First, we have Lady Fu Xuan of the Divination Commission. What do you think best suits her?

t_ch: 哈努兄弟火箭筒。
t_en: Brother Hanu Launcher.

t_ch: 符玄大人还有这种雅兴？看来小女子睡去的这段时日，仙舟真是变了。
t_en: Lady Fu Xuan has such refined taste? The Xianzhou really has changed a lot in the time I've been asleep.

t_ch: 那便依您的主意。
t_en: Let's go with your suggestion.

t_ch: 钟表眼罩。
t_en: Clockie eye mask.

t_ch: 呃…是因为能给第三只眼睛用吗？
t_en: Uhm, did you pick that because of her third eye?

t_ch: 细致入微，妙趣横生，想来是件周到又新奇的礼物，就依您的主意。
t_en: A meticulously thought out and interesting suggestion. It'll make for a novel gift. Let's go with that.

t_ch: 独轮飞车。
t_en: Unicycler.

t_ch: 呃…你能想象她开车的样子吗？
t_en: Um, can you picture her driving?

t_ch: 虽然很酷，但不合适吧？
t_en: It looks cool, but it's probably not a good idea.

t_ch: 接下来是景元将军。
t_en: Next is General Jing Yuan.

t_ch: 一点匹诺康尼股份。
t_en: Some Penacony shares.

t_ch: 这你可决定不了吧！
t_en: That's not your decision to make!

t_ch: 这倒是小女子梦寐以求的珍宝，但将军毕竟身居高位，收不得这个。
t_en: That's the kind of treasure I've always yearned for, but the general's position won't allow him to accept such a gift.

t_ch: 《美梦往事》胶卷。
t_en: Dreamjoy Memoir Film Reel.

t_ch: 「想了解盛会之星的历史，就去克劳克影视乐园」——嗯，将军会喜欢这份礼物的。
t_en: "Come to Clock Studios Theme Park to learn more about the history of the Planet of Festivities!" I think the general will like this gift.

t_ch: 送他一支奇兵。
t_en: Send him a division of elite fighters.

t_ch: 你认真的？
t_en: Seriously?

t_ch: 如今联盟有列位相助，胜过五十支奇兵，再送就边际效应啦。
t_en: Now that the Alliance has all of you there to help them, sending any elite fighters more would just be overkill.

t_ch: 最后一位…丹鼎司的白露大人，她喜好热闹。
t_en: Finally, we have Lady Bailu of the Alchemy Commission. She loves excitement.

t_ch: 苏乐达糖浆。
t_en: SoulGlad syrup.

t_ch: 好主意。虽然随处可见，但终归是匹诺康尼的名产，带些回去正合适。
t_en: That's a good idea. It might be commonplace, but it's still the specialty of Penacony. It would make for an ideal gift to bring home.

t_ch: 仓鼠球骑士发卡。
t_en: Hamster Ball Knight hair clip.

t_ch: 看您仪表堂堂，内心倒可爱得像个孩子。是白露大人会喜欢的装饰，就依您的主意啦。
t_en: You carry yourself with such dignity and grace, but you still have the heart of a child. Lady Bailu loves these types of accessories. Let's go with your suggestion.

t_ch: 天环族羽饰。
t_en: Halovian wings accessory.

t_ch: 又是角又是翅膀…要素太多了吧。
t_en: Both wings and horns? That's a little much, don't you think?

t_ch: 这便齐啦，劳烦您为我装起来吧。
t_en: That's all of them. Please wrap them up for me.

t_ch: 有二位相助，比预想中更顺利呢，算算时间，竟还剩下了些。
t_en: Thanks to both your help, everything went smoother than I expected. And it looks like we still have some time to spare.

t_ch: 要不咱们再去梦境贩售店看看？虽然外头也有忆泡，但匹诺康尼的梦泡可不一般，就算不送人，拿来留作纪念也很合适。
t_en: Why don't we pay another visit to the Dreamscape Sales Store? You can find memory bubbles pretty much anywhere, but Penacony's dream bubbles are extraordinary. Even if we don't get them as gifts, we can keep them as souvenirs for ourselves.

t_ch: 留给自己的纪念物么…的确有必要呢。
t_en: A souvenir for myself? That's definitely an idea I can get behind.

t_ch: 不过停云还有一事心愿未了。既然已是最后一站，可否借一步说话，找个僻静的角落和二位谈些私事？
t_en: But there's one more thing I wish to do. Since this is our last stop, could I have a word with you two in private? Perhaps somewhere more secluded. I'd like to discuss a personal matter.

t_ch: 停云小姐，你这是……
t_en: Miss Tingyun, what's this about?

t_ch: 小女子与各位同行多日，倍受关照，感激不尽。
t_en: You've looked out for me during our travels together these past few days. I'm truly grateful and can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 只是有些事仍难以忘怀，觉得分别前，心里话还是说开了为好。
t_en: But there are some things I find difficult to forget. I feel it's only right that I tell you what's on my mind before we part ways.

t_ch: 二位可还记得最初遇见小女子的情境？
t_en: Do you two still remember what it was like when you first saw me?

t_ch: 怎么可能忘得了？{NICKNAME}被吓得定在原地，直喊着什么——
t_en: How could we forget? {NICKNAME} was so shocked, {M#he}{F#she} was frozen in place when {M#he}{F#she} shouted...

t_ch: 停云，怎么是你？
t_en: Tingyun? How?

t_ch: 幻胧，怎么是你…
t_en: Phantylia? How...

t_ch: 花火，怎么是你！
t_en: Sparkle? How!

t_ch: 分毫不差。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 也正因如此，与列位同行的日子，小女子心头总有种别样的滋味。
t_en: And that's precisely why I haven't been able to shake off this strange feeling in the days since I started traveling with you.

t_ch: 虽然嘴上说着「敢请教二位尊姓大名」，可心里总忍不住想，这「初次见面」的景象，对于各位恐怕是「久别重逢」。
t_en: When I asked for your names, I couldn't help but think that this "first encounter" was actually a "long-awaited reunion" for you.

t_ch: 列位的仙舟之行，小女子也听了七七八八，不得不感叹那幻胧伪装之精妙，恰如世上的另一个我，骗过了所有人。
t_en: I've heard about what happened to you on the Xianzhou, and I've got to admit, that Phantylia's disguise was so perfect, it was like another me tricking everyone.

t_ch: 是啊，我们甚至都分不清停云小姐是何时被幻胧取代的。
t_en: Yeah, we couldn't even tell when you were replaced by Phantylia.

t_ch: 还以为是追捕卡芙卡那会儿，停云小姐消失了一阵，再出现时被掉包的。
t_en: I figured it happened when we were after Kafka. You vanished for a while, I thought you must have come back as a double.

t_ch: 可事实却要早得多呀。
t_en: But it was much earlier than that.

t_ch: 每每想到这里，便会感到些许落寞，仿佛小女子的人生被切走了一部分，偏偏自己还被排除在外。
t_en: That thought has filled me with sorrow, as if a piece of my life had been stolen away, and I had no part in it.

t_ch: 这同样也是我的困惑之处。幻胧是如何扮作停云的模样，我毕竟未曾亲见。
t_en: That's what I've been puzzled about too. I never actually saw how Phantylia managed to disguise herself as me.

t_ch: 依二位的意思，那时的「停云」看上去是哪般模样？
t_en: What was your impression of that "Tingyun," if you recall?

t_ch: 有点神秘。
t_en: A bit mysterious.

t_ch: 这怕是狐人天性，谁都知道神秘是吸引人的窍门喔。
t_en: That's just the nature of the foxians. It's widely known that mystery is the key to our allure.

t_ch: 有点危险。
t_en: A bit dangerous.

t_ch: 小女子明明手无缚鸡之力，莫非说的是心思活络？
t_en: But I'm not very strong physically. Are you referring to my quick wits?

t_ch: 有点精明。
t_en: A bit calculating.

t_ch: 这点倒没错呢，但小女子向来只算计事，从不算计人。
t_en: That part is true. I'm always calculating when it comes to business, but never with people.

t_ch: 那看上去，又像是何许人？
t_en: So, what did she seem like to you?

t_ch: 圆滑周到的商人。
t_en: A shrewd merchant.

t_ch: 哎呀，小女子就当这是夸赞吧，下回您不妨说是「八面玲珑」。
t_en: I'll just assume that's a compliment! Maybe next time, you could just say "silver-tongued."

t_ch: 毁灭令使潜力股。
t_en: A potential Emanator of the Destruction.

t_ch: 这…算是恭维？怕是有些担待不起啊。
t_en: Is that... a compliment? I'm not sure if I'm deserving of that title.

t_ch: 那…可有印象深刻之处？
t_en: Was there anything particularly memorable?

t_ch: 情商很高。
t_en: High EQ.

t_ch: 这是大实话，连我都很想学呢。
t_en: That's true! I'd love to learn how you do it!

t_ch: 柔韧性很好。
t_en: Extremely flexible.

t_ch: 现在不是开这种玩笑的时候啦……
t_en: Now's not the time for those kinds of jokes...

t_ch: 三月七说你好会差遣人。
t_en: March 7th said you were bossy.

t_ch: 你…别当面告状呀！
t_en: Hey! No tattling!

t_ch: 那么最后一个问题。
t_en: Now, the final question.

t_ch: 在两位看来，小女子与记忆中的那人…称得上别无二致吗？
t_en: In your eyes, am I just like the Tingyun you remember?

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 这不重要。
t_en: That's not important.

t_ch: 等等——这说法是不是有点怪？明明停云小姐才是本尊，为什么要和冒牌货比较？
t_en: Wait — don't you find that question a bit weird? You're the real Tingyun. Why compare yourself to an imposter?

t_ch: 三月小姐怕是误会了，小女子只是觉着有些惋惜，又有些庆幸。
t_en: Miss March, you might have the wrong idea. I'm just feeling a mix of regret and relief.
...Huh?

t_ch: 做生意的总要消息灵通些，小女子在返航前，必然会知晓罗浮近况。
t_en: As a merchant, staying well-informed is crucial. It's only natural for me to stay updated on the events of the Luofu before I return.

t_ch: 若回到当初，仓促来去，想必也帮不上什么忙。我应当会暂缓行程，在外寻求盟友的帮助，可如此一来……
t_en: If I had rushed in and out back then, I wouldn't have been much help. Instead, I would've postponed the trip and sought assistance from allies, but in that case...

t_ch: 我们岂不是根本就不会相见？
t_en: We wouldn't have crossed paths.

t_ch: 是呀。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 听二位描述，那绝灭大君果真本领非凡，所作所为都和小女子本人如出一辙。说是伪装，更像是代我活过了几日。
t_en: Based on your description, that Lord Ravager was indeed cunning. Her demeanor mirrored mine so closely that it felt like she was living my life for a few days rather than merely disguising herself.

t_ch: 各位的仙舟之旅，小女子遗憾错失。可后续种种展开，除却阴谋的部分，却又与我本人在场不差分毫。停云因此看见了一种可能……
t_en: I regret missing out on your Xianzhou journey, but everything that unfolded afterward, aside from the scheming, played out exactly as if I'd been there. This leads me to consider another possibility...

t_ch: 那幻胧夺走了我的人生，狐人睚眦必报，绝不吃亏，小女子一定得从她手里拿回些什么才是。
t_en: Phantylia had stolen a piece of my life, and a foxian always gets even. I will not be taken advantage of. I must take something back from her as well.

t_ch: 但思来想去，可称得上等价之物的…也只有「和各位的缘分」了。
t_en: But after thinking it over, the only thing that could be considered equal value... is the bond I have with all of you.

t_ch: 想到这里，小女子才释然了许多，也总算明白了该如何向二位开口。
t_en: That thought has brought me great comfort, and now I know how to speak to you properly.

t_ch: 各位恩人与我素未谋面，却助我于水火之中，此情此景，与那时有何不同？小女子本该以礼相待，却因忌惮那幻胧，反变得不像自己了。
t_en: We've never even met before, yet here you are, offering your help, just as before. I should've treated you with respect, but fear of Phantylia made me act like somebody else.

t_ch: 而现在，停云便要将失去的都拿回来，就从一个理所应当的称呼开始——
t_en: And now, I will reclaim everything I have lost. And it should begin with a proper acknowledgment—

t_ch: 各位恩公，停云向你们郑重道谢。
t_en: Dear benefactors, I extend my heartfelt gratitude to you.

t_ch: 不用谢。
t_en: You're welcome.

t_ch: 还是有点恍惚。
t_en: I'm still a bit confused.

t_ch: 该调整身份认知了。
t_en: It's time to rethink how you see yourself.

t_ch: 看来是个不错的开头嘛。今后，小女子还会做出更多类似的尝试。
t_en: Looks like we're off to a good start! Moving forward, I'll definitely be trying more things like this from now on.

t_ch: 届时，列位恩公便是停云心间的勇气呢。
t_en: And you, my dear benefactors, will be my source of courage.

t_ch: 太夸张啦，停云小姐可千万别做危险的决定，{M#他}{F#她}担不起！
t_en: Miss Tingyun, that's way too over the top. Just don't make any risky decisions. It might overwhelm {M#him}{F#her}!

t_ch: 这些都是后话了。只愿从今以后，留在恩公们记忆里的是「停云」，而不再是「幻胧」啦。
t_en: Haha, we'll see. I just hope the Tingyun in your memories won't be tainted by Phantylia anymore.

t_ch: 小女子说完了，希望没有耽误太多时间，我们接着上路吧。
t_en: Alright. No more questions. I hope I didn't take up too much of your time. Let's continue on our way.

t_ch: 欢迎光临梦境贩售店，爱德华医生竭诚为您服务！
t_en: Welcome to the Dreamscape Sales Store! Dr. Edward at your service!

t_ch: 我们来买几个梦送人。要有异域风情，最好还能让人意犹未尽。
t_en: We're looking for some dreams to give as gifts — something exotic and, preferably, captivating.

t_ch: 我有必要提醒一下——过多的要求不能帮您筛选作品，只会提前破坏您的心情。
t_en: Just a friendly reminder: Being too picky won't help you find the best dreams. It'll just dampen your spirits ahead of time.

t_ch: 不过，总有运气特别好的顾客。前不久，我刚收到一枚完美符合条件的梦泡。
t_en: That being said, there are always some lucky exceptions. Not long ago, I procured a dream bubble that sounds perfect for you.

t_ch: 故事发生在仙舟联盟，一位年少成名的剑客欲摘得剑首之名，而他刚满百岁的狐人师妹却在此时令他陷入了两难之境……
t_en: The story unfolds in the Xianzhou Alliance, where a young sword master strives to become the Sword Champion. However, his junior foxian soror who's just turned a hundred, puts him in a tough spot...

t_ch: 怎么有点耳熟又有点陌生……
t_en: Why does that sound oddly familiar...

t_ch: 毕竟是要带回仙舟，还是挑些和联盟无关的吧。您随意选几个便好。
t_en: Since I'm returning to the Xianzhou, I think it's better to choose something unrelated to the Alliance. Feel free to pick a few for me.

t_ch: 爱德华医生最喜欢您这种…嗯，淡然自若的客人。
t_en: I love customers like you... So easy to work with.

t_ch: 你是想说好糊弄吧……
t_en: So easy to take advantage of, you mean?

t_ch: 可不许挑那种很奇怪的啊。
t_en: Just don't pick anything weird, okay?

t_ch: 对啦，提及梦境，我也有一份薄礼想赠予恩公。
t_en: Oh, speaking of dreamscapes, I have a gift for you, my benefactors.

t_ch: 连日叨扰，眼看着将要分别，总不能毫无表示。小女子为列位恩公留下一枚梦泡，希望能拿得出手。
t_en: After troubling you all this time, I couldn't leave without properly expressing my thanks. Here's a dream bubble for you — I hope you enjoy it.

t_ch: 哇…当然可以，太客气啦。
t_en: Wow, that's really nice of you!

t_ch: 这是为您准备的梦泡，请收好。期待您下次光临。
t_en: Here are the dream bubbles I've prepared for you. Thank you for your business!

t_ch: 那么，事情总算办妥了。算算时间，也该回去了？
t_en: Well, it looks like everything's finally in order. I suppose it's time to head back?

t_ch: 欢迎，您想体验一个什么滋味的梦境？
t_en: Welcome! What kind of dreamscape would you like to experience today?

t_ch: 看看停云赠予的梦。
t_en: Look at the dream gifted by Tingyun.

t_ch: 你将停云赠予的梦泡交给了对方。
t_en: You handed over the dream bubble gifted by Tingyun.

t_ch: 喔…这可真是场精彩的梦，波澜起伏，要素齐全。这位客人，我愿意高价回收，您可以考虑下。
t_en: Oh... this is quite the dream—full of twists and turns, with all the right elements. I'd be willing to buy it back at a high price. Think about it.

t_ch: 看完再说。
t_en: I'll let you know after I've seen it.

t_ch: 别想了，不卖。
t_en: Hard pass.

t_ch: 那么，请您闭上眼睛——
t_en: Then, please close your eyes—

t_ch: 如往常那般，梦泡顺畅地向你让渡着纷繁的情境——
t_en: As usual, the dream bubble smoothly presents you with a myriad of scenes—

t_ch: 然而，这场梦你却非常熟悉，那几乎就是你曾经历过的罗浮之行。
t_en: However, you're very familiar with this dream. It's almost the same as your travels on the Luofu.

t_ch: 唯一的区别是，与你同行的狐人女子停云，从未遭遇绝灭大君，而是以自己的意志，协助你解决了那场危机。
t_en: The only difference is that the foxian lady, Tingyun, who was travelling with you, never encountered the Lord Ravager. Instead, she helped you solve that crisis of her own free will.

t_ch: 你拥有一份类似的记忆。
t_en: You have a similar memory.

t_ch: 但你还是决定留下这场梦。
t_en: But you decide to keep this dream.

t_ch: 还没想好。
t_en: I haven't decided yet.

t_ch: 先前已经看了一批礼物，只剩下三位没有着落。不过方才耽搁了不少时间，怕是不能再慢悠悠地晃荡啦……
t_en: I already picked up a few gifts earlier, so that means there's only three more left to go. But we've already lost a lot of time, so we'd better not drag our feet...

t_ch: 这里便好。
t_en: This is a good spot.

t_ch: 要送停云小姐回去了…有点舍不得啊。
t_en: I can't believe it's time for Miss Tingyun to leave. I'm not ready to say goodbye just yet.

t_ch: 你们回来了？
t_en: You're back!

t_ch: 嗯，遇上点事，耽搁了一会儿。具体…还是等杨叔回来解释吧。
t_en: Yeah, we got delayed on the way. Mr. Yang will explain in detail when he gets back.

t_ch: 仙舟使者已经到了吗？
t_en: Has the messenger from the Xianzhou arrived yet?

t_ch: 嗯，已经聊过一会儿了，正巧是你们的熟人。
t_en: Yes, we've been chatting for a while. Turns out, she's an old acquaintance of yours.

t_ch: 各位，别来无恙。
t_en: Long time no see, everyone.

t_ch: 驭空大人？
t_en: Madam Yukong?

t_ch: 其实我反复设想过现在的情景。
t_en: I've imagined this moment many times.

t_ch: 担心自己会在众目睽睽下失态，甚至想清楚了要说的每一句话。
t_en: I was worried I might embarrass myself in front of you. I even rehearsed every word beforehand.

t_ch: 但看来毫无必要。
t_en: But now it seems it was unnecessary.

t_ch: 我来接你回去了，停云。
t_en: Tingyun, I'm here to take you home.

t_ch: 我们应当暂且回避下。两位久别重逢，先好好聊聊吧。
t_en: You two must have a lot to catch up on after being apart for so long. We'll give you some space.

t_ch: 没想到来的是驭空大人，真叫人又惊又喜……
t_en: Madam Yukong, I wasn't expecting it to be you. What a wonderful surprise!

t_ch: 是我主动要求的，毕竟…回来的人是你。
t_en: I wanted to come because... after all, it's you who's coming home.

t_ch: 是啊，偏偏是我……
t_en: Yeah, me, of all people...

t_ch: 你的身体…往后的日子会很辛苦吧？
t_en: Your body... It's going to be tough from now on, isn't it?

t_ch: 都在鬼门关走过一遭了，还有什么可挑剔的呢？
t_en: I've already escaped death once. What more could I ask for?

t_ch: 也不全是坏处，现在和驭空大人掰掰腕子，兴许赢的会是我？
t_en: Also, it's not all bad. I might even beat you at arm wrestling now.

t_ch: 你还是和过去一样坦然。
t_en: You're still the same — carefree as always.

t_ch: 我想说的话有很多，但都留到路上吧。回罗浮路途遥远，我们有的是时间。
t_en: I have a lot I'd like to tell you, but I'll save it for the road. It's a long journey back to the Luofu.

t_ch: 就算无法根除，缓解病症的法子…仙舟一定拿得出手。
t_en: Even if we can't completely rid you of the pain, there must be ways to alleviate it on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 这我从不怀疑，只是…既无旁人，停云便多问一句。
t_en: I have no doubt. But... since we're alone here, may I ask you one more thing?

t_ch: …非得回罗浮么？
t_en: ...Do I really have to go back to the Luofu?

t_ch: …怎么？你不打算回去吗？
t_en: ...Why? You don't want to?

t_ch: 幻胧处事心狠手辣，可最该被除掉的我却活了下来，阮•梅大人与仙舟素无干系，又为何要对小女子出手相救？
t_en: Despite Phantylia's ruthlessness, she spared me when I should have been eliminated. And why did Miss Ruan Mei, who has no ties to the Xianzhou, go out of her way to save me?

t_ch: 想来是停云在不知不觉间成了他人对弈的棋子吧。
t_en: It seems as if I've become a pawn in someone else's game.

t_ch: …联盟内部也有人这么认为，嫌疑人如今正在「玉阙」。
t_en: ...There are people within the Alliance who also believe that the suspect is now on the Yuque.

t_ch: 如此这般，停云实在不该重返罗浮，为局势平添混乱。不妨顺水推舟，物尽其用？
t_en: If that's the case, it's all the more reason for me not to return to the Luofu and complicate things. I might as well take advantage of this situation.

t_ch: 物尽其用…是何意？
t_en: Take advantage of the situation? What do you mean?

t_ch: 如今{RUBY_B#毁灭}烬灭{RUBY_E#}的烙印在我体内维持着微妙的平衡，并且…它们是由幻胧亲手所造。
t_en: Right now, the marks of {RUBY_B#Destruction}Ruin{RUBY_E#} inside me maintain a delicate balance. Moreover... they were created by Phantylia herself.

t_ch: 如果可以，希望驭空大人将此事上呈将军，送我觐见元帅——这副身躯，也许能帮助联盟向着仙舟的敌人更近一步。
t_en: If possible, please inform the general and arrange for me to meet with the marshal. Maybe this body of mine can help the Alliance get one step closer to the Xianzhou's enemies.

t_ch: 此前阮•梅只身造访罗浮，也提到了你的事，与星穹列车发来消息几乎是同一时间。
t_en: When Ruan Mei visited the Luofu earlier, she mentioned what happened to you. This coincided with when the message sent by the Astral Express arrived.

t_ch: 天将们判断，你的生还不在幻胧计划之内，若运用得当，必能作为一支奇兵，帮联盟取回先机。
t_en: The Arbiter-Generals concluded that Phantylia did not intend for you to survive. If utilized strategically, this could give the Alliance an element of surprise.

t_ch: 但如此一来，必须对内隐瞒你「死而复生」的消息。我实在希望做决定前，能听听你自己的想法。若你想要回家……
t_en: If we follow this plan, we must keep your "resurrection" hidden. But before making any decisions, I want to know what you think. If you wish to go home...

t_ch: 那我定会力排众议。
t_en: I'll support you, no matter what others may say.

t_ch: 还好，停云也算替您省了些力排众议的口舌。
t_en: Then I'm glad. I shall save you the trouble of hearing what those people may say.

t_ch: 前路凶险，你若打定主意要走，我自然没道理阻止。但…许是我私心过甚吧，我仍希望能清楚地知道，你的选择是否出于本心？
t_en: The road ahead is perilous. If you decide to take it, I won't stop you. Maybe I'm being selfish, but I still want to know — are you choosing this because it's what you really want?

t_ch: 俗话说因祸得福，恰恰是这趟美梦之旅让停云看清了自己。
t_en: I've come to see myself clearly in this dream journey. Perhaps it's what people call a blessing in disguise.

t_ch: 而此时此刻，也有一桩旁证能为驭空大人展现我的本心。
t_en: There is someone who can attest to my true intentions.

t_ch: 请跟我来，想必就在这附近。
t_en: Please follow me. She should be around here somewhere.

t_ch: 那是……
t_en: Is that...

t_ch: 此前，因为一场奇遇，我在梦中碎成了许多个「停云」。她们都是我的一部分，专注于不同的记忆。
t_en: After a strange encounter, I was fragmented into multiple "Tingyuns," each embodying a different aspect of my memories.

t_ch: 而其中一位，当时她走得太过遥远，以致未能回顾此身，直到现在。
t_en: And one of them hasn't returned to me because she ventured too far. Until now.

t_ch: …你说的是她么？
t_en: ...You mean her?

t_ch: 她这是…在做什么？
t_en: What is she... doing?

t_ch: 她正沉醉于某件令我无比迷恋的事物。
t_en: She is captivated by something that has deeply mesmerized me.

t_ch: 作为一道心绪，她和新生的孩子没有区别。登上这艘船，仅仅是出于内心的悸动。
t_en: As a fleeting emotion, she's no different from a newborn. Her boarding this ship was solely down to her inner impulse.

t_ch: 驭空大人，还记得么？也许对您而言，那只是一次平平无奇的飞行。可对于某个孩子……
t_en: Madam Yukong, do you remember? Perhaps that was just an ordinary flight for you. But for a certain child...

t_ch: 那是她与「天空」的初次邂逅。
t_en: That was her first experience with the "sky."

t_ch: 我当然记得。
t_en: Of course I remember.

t_ch: 那时，你还是能被我抱在怀里的年纪。
t_en: Back then, you were still young enough for me to carry.

t_ch: 现在也未尝不可，您试试无妨。
t_en: You probably still can! Want to give it a try?

t_ch: 怕是抱不动了。
t_en: I'm afraid you'd be too heavy for me now.

t_ch: 开个玩笑。
t_en: Just kidding.

t_ch: 回想起来，那会儿你总缠着我要上一次星槎看看，还把飞行士当作自己毕生的理想。可真正飞上高空后，却又惊慌失措，紧紧抓着旁人的衣角不放。
t_en: I remember you kept begging me to take you to the starskiff and even dreamed of becoming a pilot. But once you were airborne, you were so nervous that you clung to others for dear life.

t_ch: 可正是那次飞行，撬动了我的一生。
t_en: This flight was a turning point in my life.

t_ch: 彼时，眼见天地铺开，不见边际，我深深为此着迷，不是因为站在高处，而是意识到了一件事——
t_en: The boundless cosmos unfolded before me. I was completely mesmerized, not just by the view, but by a realization—

t_ch: 在这世上，我们还能走得更远。
t_en: In this world, there are paths beyond waiting for us to explore.

t_ch: 因此，即便没有驾驶星槎的天分，性格也不好争斗，小女子仍选择随商团四处奔走。
t_en: Even though I lacked the talent to pilot the starskiff and wasn't much of a warrior, I still chose to travel around with the merchant guild.

t_ch: 时至今日，这份雀跃也依然在心中鼓动。
t_en: Even to this day, this thrill still courses through me.

t_ch: 仍未改变？
t_en: That hasn't changed?

t_ch: 从未改变。
t_en: Never.

t_ch: 如今，停云也能再次启程，以一介「忘归之人」的身份，行向更远的天际了。
t_en: Now, I can embark once more on my journey as a "Traveler of No Return," exploring the paths that lie beyond.

t_ch: 话虽如此，「鸣火」那边还好吗？
t_en: Speaking of which, how are things with the Whistling Flames?

t_ch: 我暂交给岩明代为打理了。你也了解他的为人，不必担心。只是此人办事作风总有些激进，恐怕不太符合你一贯的行商原则。
t_en: I've left Yanming in charge for now. He's reliable, but he can be a bit extreme, and might not always follow your principles as a traveling merchant.

t_ch: 行商原则……
t_en: My principles...

t_ch: 「舍其所争、取其所弃，方能交流天下、得我所欲」——八面玲珑这点，我今后也不会改变。
t_en: "Forsake what others fight for and take what others abandon. That's the way to navigate the world and achieve my goals." I will continue to uphold my principles.

t_ch: 但如今，停云同样明白，行商的法则能与人博弈，却难与神相争。
t_en: But now, I've also learned that while these merchant principles may apply to humans, they falter in the face of the gods.

t_ch: 这原本就不是你该承担的责任。你只是这场战争里一个无辜的幸存者。
t_en: But this shouldn't fall on your shoulders to begin with. You are simply an innocent survivor in this war.

t_ch: 驭空大人…不也当过战争的「幸存者」吗？
t_en: Madam Yukong, you were once a "survivor" in the war too, weren't you?

t_ch: 朦胧的梦中，好似有人对我说过：幸存者的名字往往是「不幸」，可是……
t_en: In a hazy dream, I vaguely remember someone telling me that the inescapable price of survival is often more adversity, but...

t_ch: 驭空大人，天上煌煌众神以人为弈，博戏群星，留下一地冤魂孤冢、白骨累累…您可曾想过，要让祂们付出代价？
t_en: Madam Yukong, the gods use humans as pawns in their cosmic game, sacrificing countless innocent souls... Have you ever thought about making THEM pay?

t_ch: …每时每刻。可如我们这样的微尘又怎么逃得开成为代价的棋局呢？
t_en: ...Every moment of every day. But as inconsequential as we are, how can we escape the fate of being mere pawns?

t_ch: 如果可以选择，我真不愿你做什么棋子。我只愿你仍是那快活的小狐狸，不必掺和进这场战争中……
t_en: If I could choose, I would never want you to become a pawn. I just wish you could still be that carefree little fox, not caught up in this war...

t_ch: 别那么悲观嘛，驭空大人。
t_en: Don't be disheartened, Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 承蒙大君「厚爱」，这回停云做得了局中人。
t_en: Thanks to the "favor" of the Lord Ravager, I've become a player in this game.

t_ch: 要知道，策杖独行、蚯蚓降龙…能让将帅无计可施的，总是些无名的小卒子。
t_en: Remember, it's often the unassuming pawns — the lone traveler or the worm that subdues the dragon — that leave even the best strategists helpless.

t_ch: 所以如今，停云也要当一粒狡猾的灰尘，让那些幕后大手……
t_en: So now, I'll become a cunning speck of dust and make those masterminds behind the curtains...

t_ch: 好好打上几个喷嚏啦。
t_en: Sneeze a few times!

t_ch: 同一时间 筑梦边境
t_en: Meanwhile at Dream's Edge

t_ch: 这里也有「秩序」的影响？
t_en: Is this place also affected by the Order?

t_ch: 不，这一站只是为了告别。我中途改变了某个决定，此行变数太多……
t_en: No, I'm just here to bid farewell. I changed my mind along the way. This journey has too many variables...

t_ch: 再添一桩也无妨。
t_en: So it shouldn't matter if we add another.

t_ch: 你怎么知道她在这儿？
t_en: How did you know she would be here?

t_ch: 我不知道，只是心里有几个猜想，碰碰运气。看来我也有交好运的时候。
t_en: I didn't. I had a hunch and decided to try my luck. It seems I can be lucky sometimes.

t_ch: 把这种事交给运气，不像你的作为。
t_en: It's not like you to leave things to luck.

t_ch: 我也在试着改变。
t_en: I'm trying to change, too.

t_ch: 我想旁人应该回避这种场合。去吧。
t_en: I don't think you need a bystander in this scenario. Go on ahead.

t_ch: 你不担心我借机逃脱？
t_en: You're not worried I'll take the opportunity to escape?

t_ch: 我相信你是个有能力，也有意愿利用一切的人，但那其中绝不包含知更鸟小姐。
t_en: I believe you're the kind of person that has the ability and desire to use everything to your advantage... but that "everything" does not include Miss Robin.

t_ch: …谢谢。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 那万维克先生……
t_en: Now then, Mr. Wonweek...

t_ch: 我不去，凭什么？
t_en: I'm not leaving. No way.

t_ch: 让他留下吧，不会碍事的。
t_en: Let him stay. He won't be in the way.

t_ch: 况且，我也需要万维克在场。无论如何，她都不应该和一名逃犯有接触。
t_en: I actually need Wonweek to be there. She shouldn't be seen talking to a fugitive no matter the circumstances.

t_ch: 你…不打算在她面前亮明真身？
t_en: You... don't intend to reveal your true identity to her?

t_ch: 一场道别未必需要双方都知晓。
t_en: Both parties don't have to be aware that this is a farewell.

t_ch: …她不该为我的私心承担任何风险。
t_en: ...I shouldn't put her at risk for my selfishness.

t_ch: …请便。
t_en: ...As you wish.

t_ch: 那咱们上前去？
t_en: Shall we go then?

t_ch: （深呼吸）……
t_en: (deep breath)...

t_ch: 走吧。如果我不能迈出这一步…启程也无从谈起。
t_en: Let's go. Our journey can't begin if I can't take this first step.

t_ch: 没想到会在这儿遇见您，知更鸟…女士。
t_en: I didn't expect to see you here, Miss Robin.

t_ch: 您好啊，女士。请问怎么称呼？
t_en: It's nice to meet you, Miss. May I know your name?

t_ch: 我叫…万维克，是隐夜鸫家系的成员。
t_en: My name is... Wonweek. I'm a member of the Nightingale Family.

t_ch: 原来是一位筑梦师，感谢你们的辛苦付出，梦境才能从危机中恢复过来。
t_en: Oh, you're a Dreamweaver. Thank you for all your hard work. You all are the reason the Dreamscape has been able to recover from this crisis.

t_ch: 知更鸟女士才是，这阵子一定很忙吧，怎么会来筑梦边境呢？
t_en: You're too kind, Miss Robin. You must be very busy. What brings you here to Dream's Edge?

t_ch: 忙里偷闲，想找个安静的地方散心，最好能看见星星。走着走着就到了这里。
t_en: I'm taking a break. I wanted to find a quiet place to relax, ideally somewhere where I could see the stars. Before I knew it, I'd walked all the way here.

t_ch: 虽然只是梦中的星空呢。
t_en: I know these stars only exist in the dream but...

t_ch: 关于这一点，也许梦境和现实没有区别。
t_en: Well, perhaps the dreamscape is not so different from reality.

t_ch: 「我们抬头看向夜空，但星星不在那里。我们看到的每一束光，都是它们很久以前的模样。」
t_en: "When we look up into the night sky, the stars aren't really there. The glimmering lights we see are merely the images of stars from long ago."

t_ch: 我们何曾见过真实的星空？
t_en: So, have we ever truly seen a real sky full of stars?

t_ch: 万维克小姐说话很有哲理呢，像是我认识的一个人。
t_en: You're very philosophical, Miss Wonweek. You remind me of someone I know.

t_ch: 您在这附近工作吗？
t_en: Do you work nearby?

t_ch: 没有没有。今天是休息日，待会儿有场演出，我提前来找个好位置。
t_en: No, no. Today is my rest day. There's going to be a show later, so I came early to find a good seat.

t_ch: 演出？在这里吗？
t_en: A show? Here?

t_ch: 在更远的地方，据说是一出谢幕剧，比起现场观看，更适合在远处聆听。
t_en: It's somewhere farther away. They say it's a farewell performance, the kind that's better to listen to from afar than watch it performed live.

t_ch: 这样啊，那瞭望台是个不错的选择。从这儿望去，能看见整个匹诺康尼。
t_en: I see. In that case, the observation deck is a good choice. You can see all of Penacony from here.

t_ch: 距离会让一些事物变得更美丽。
t_en: Distance can make some things appear more beautiful.

t_ch: 知更鸟女士，演出开场前，我能留下来和您一同看星星吗？
t_en: Miss Robin, may I stay here with you to gaze at the stars, before the show begins?

t_ch: 当然啦。您太客气了，该请求的是我才对。这里是筑梦边境，造梦者才是它的主人。
t_en: Of course! You don't have to be so courteous. I should be the one asking. This is Dream's Edge, after all, and the Dreamweavers are its true masters.

t_ch: 话虽如此，这些星星有些陌生，没有一颗是熟悉的。让人感到不安。
t_en: Still, these stars are unfamiliar to me. I don't recognize any of them, and it makes me feel uneasy.

t_ch: 是什么让您不安呢？是天空的高远吗，还是未知？
t_en: What makes you feel this way? Is it because the sky is so far away, or because it's the unknown?

t_ch: 也许两者皆有。
t_en: Perhaps it's both.

t_ch: 尽管星核之灾没有造成实际影响，但还是有许多住客选择退房，酒店冷清了许多。不知匹诺康尼的明天会如何，希望不会影响知更鸟女士的生活。
t_en: The Stellaron Disaster may not have had any real consequences, but many guests still chose to check out. Now, the hotel feels quieter than before. It makes me wonder what Penacony's future will be like. I just hope it will not negatively affect you, Miss Robin.

t_ch: 谢谢，我没事啦。阴差阳错，在这场灾难中看见了许多过去，也想清了很多未来。
t_en: You're too kind. I'll be fine. The disaster was unexpected, but because of it, I was able to witness many pasts and think about many futures.

t_ch: 唯一称得上遗憾的，只是有些话没来得及说出口。
t_en: My only real regret is that I left some things unsaid.

t_ch: 是谈话的对象离去了么？
t_en: Did the person you wanted to say them to leave?

t_ch: 嗯，他走得匆忙，甚至没来得及说声再见。
t_en: Yes. He left in such a hurry that I didn't even get the chance to say goodbye.

t_ch: 真心不需要太多修饰，也许你的心意已经传达到了。
t_en: Sometimes what's in your heart doesn't need to be dressed up in words. Maybe he already knows what you wanted to say.

t_ch: 但想要鼓励一个人的时候，还是得多花些心思才对吧？
t_en: But don't you think we should put in some effort when we want to encourage someone?

t_ch: 冒昧问一句，您想要鼓励的人…是谁呢？
t_en: If you don't mind me asking, who is it that you wish to encourage?

t_ch: 是一位刚刚经历失败，将要踏上远行的人。
t_en: It's someone who experienced failure recently and is about to embark on a long journey.

t_ch: 这样的人在匹诺康尼有许多。对逐梦客而言，失败就像是夕阳，每天都会到来。
t_en: There are many such people on Penacony. Failure is like the setting sun to a Dreamchaser. It's something that happens every day.

t_ch: 相信以他们的毅力，只需要知更鸟女士一个微笑，就能重新来过吧。
t_en: I believe they all have the willpower to start anew, especially after receiving a smile from you, Miss Robin.

t_ch: 但那个人不一样，他比普通人坚强许多，人们因此产生了一种错觉：即便他倒下了，也会比别人更快站起来。
t_en: But he's different. He's stronger than most ordinary people, but because of it, people have this misconception: They think that when he falls, he will stand back up faster than anyone else.

t_ch: 你想说，事实并非如此吗？
t_en: Are you saying that's not true?

t_ch: 他不会。在站起来前…他会犹豫这是否正确，并为此苦恼很久。
t_en: It's not. Because before he stands up again, he will hesitate and agonize for ages over whether that is the right course of action.

t_ch: 对于这类人，也许失败不失为一桩好事。反省是种可贵的品质。
t_en: Failure may be a good thing for someone like that. Being able to reflect on one's actions is a valuable quality to have.

t_ch: 可能是这样没错，但我还是希望那个人不要变。
t_en: You may be right, but I still hope that he'll never change.

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why's that?

t_ch: 因为我相信自己记忆中的他。
t_en: Because I have faith in the person I remember him to be.

t_ch: 在那场失败中，他或许采用了错误的手段，但我不觉得他抱有错误的初衷。我们曾站在同一个起点，现在轮到他飞向群星了。我相信他能找到正确的路，即便孤身一人。
t_en: He may have taken the wrong approach when he failed, but I don't think he had the wrong intentions. We once stood together at the same starting line, but now it's his turn to fly for the stars. I believe he'll find the right path, even if he has to do it alone.

t_ch: 我们不必一起飞上天际。
t_en: We don't have to soar into the sky together.

t_ch: 因为重逢一定会到来，就在那高高的云端上。
t_en: Because I know we'll reunite one day, up there in the clouds.

t_ch: 希望如此。
t_en: I hope so.

t_ch: 万维克女士，能请您帮个忙吗？这里有一幅画，在离开前，我想完成它。
t_en: Miss Wonweek, may I ask a favor? There's a jigsaw here I'd like to finish before I leave.

t_ch: 如果有筑梦师帮忙，也许就能补上空缺的部分了。
t_en: If I have a Dreamweaver's help, maybe I'll be able to fill in the missing pieces.

t_ch: 筑梦拼图么？我尽力而为。
t_en: A Dream Jigsaw? I see. I'll do my best.

t_ch: 没关系，找不到的话，就当是一起散步吧。
t_en: Don't worry if you can't find them. Just think of it as us taking a walk together.

t_ch: 看来今天，我们都受好运眷顾。
t_en: It seems we've been blessed with good fortune today.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: Is this...

t_ch: 一张字迹潦草的纸条被留在这里。
t_en: A scrawled note was left here.

t_ch: 「愿你的天空永远星光灿烂。」
t_en: "May your sky be forever radiant."

t_ch: 好熟悉啊……
t_en: It seems familiar...

t_ch: 或许是其他筑梦师留下的吧。是这个行业的一种习惯，他们希望用祝福来为梦境奠基。
t_en: Another Dreamweaver probably left this behind. It's a habit for this profession. They hope to use these blessings as a foundation for the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 啊！知更鸟女士！我是您的粉丝啊！
t_en: Oh, Miss Robin! I'm a big fan!

t_ch: 虽然不是每场演唱会都能到场，但至少也会把录像看一遍。我喜欢您的每一首歌——真想把这件事告诉全世界！
t_en: I don't get to attend every single one of your concerts, but I watch the recordings of each of them at least once. I love all of your songs! Gosh, I wish I could tell the whole world about this!

t_ch: 谢谢你的支持，我会继续加油的。
t_en: Thank you for your support. I'll keep doing my best.

t_ch: 第二块拼图。这是一双翅膀，还是用羽毛做成的装饰？
t_en: The second jigsaw piece. Is this a pair of wings, or a decoration made of feathers?

t_ch: 也许介于两者之间，这是一对折断的羽翼。
t_en: Maybe it's somewhere in between the two. It's a pair of broken wings.

t_ch: …还是再找找吧。
t_en: ...We should keep looking.

t_ch: 没关系，用它就好。
t_en: It's okay. Let's use this one.

t_ch: 「愿你的舞台永远明光幻彩。」
t_en: "May your stage be forever lustrous."

t_ch: 「另外，身材管理是很重要，但要记得好好吃饭。」
t_en: "One more thing, staying fit is important, but don't forget to eat well, too."

t_ch: 完成了，请您看看吧。
t_en: It's done. Have a look.

t_ch: 那幅画像是一种祝福，也像是某个人的决意：即便折断的羽翼不再属于天际，它将坠落的方向也是群星——
t_en: That painting feels like a blessing, and also seems to mirror someone's resolve: Even when the broken wings no longer belong to the sky, the direction in which they fall will still be towards the stars—

t_ch: 「我将要前往的那些星星，太陌生了，没有一颗是熟悉的。」
t_en: "Soon, I shall travel to those unfamiliar stars, none of which I recognize."

t_ch: 「但只要你仍在这里遥望它们——」
t_en: "But as long as you continue to gaze upon them from here—"

t_ch: 「那陌生的群星也会成为我们重逢的故乡。」
t_en: "Then those unfamiliar stars will one day be the place of our reunion."

t_ch: 最后也不舍得说声再见么？也许我比你想的要更坚强些呢……
t_en: You still couldn't bear to say goodbye in the end? Maybe I'm stronger than you think...

t_ch: 远行的路上，一定要试着让自己轻松些啊……
t_en: You must try to relax on your long journey...

t_ch: …哥哥。
t_en: ...Brother.

t_ch: 不像是完美的告别。
t_en: That didn't seem like a perfect goodbye.

t_ch: 已经足够了。我本来只想在她身边静静待会儿。
t_en: It's enough. I only meant to stay quietly by her side for a bit.

t_ch: 但你为什么要做多余的事？
t_en: But why did you do those unnecessary things?

t_ch: 你指什么？
t_en: What things?

t_ch: 那两张字条，如果被人发现，会变成对她不利的证据。
t_en: Those two notes. If someone finds them, they can be used as evidence against her.

t_ch: 哼，你都擅作主张多少次了，我就不行？
t_en: Hmph, so you can do whatever you want as many times as you want, but I can't?

t_ch: 我也不想抱憾终身啊。
t_en: I don't want to live with regret for the rest of my life either.

t_ch: 抱憾终身……
t_en: Live with regret...

t_ch: 这场告别该结束了，对吧？
t_en: We're almost done saying our goodbyes, right?

t_ch: 嗯，只剩最后一站了。
t_en: Yes, we only have one last stop.

t_ch: 但在出发前，瓦尔特先生，我还想请教您一个问题。
t_en: But before we leave, I have a question I'd like to ask you, Mr. Yang.

t_ch: 我好奇，您为何会成为一位无名客？
t_en: I'm curious. Why did you become a Nameless?

t_ch: 为什么突然问这个？
t_en: Why the sudden interest?

t_ch: 我始终有种感觉，您似乎不会放弃任何一位需要帮助的人。这一路同行下来，我更确信了这点。
t_en: I always had a feeling that you would never give up on someone who needed help. After spending time with you, I'm even more convinced of this assessment.

t_ch: 此时此刻，天上还有无数世界在等待星穹列车到访。可您和同伴停留在这里，只是为了一位素未谋面的故人，和一位声名狼藉的逃犯。
t_en: At this very moment, there are countless worlds above the skies still waiting for the arrival of the Astral Express, yet you and your companions have chosen to stay here. All for an "old friend" you never knew and a notorious fugitive.

t_ch: 「开拓」的本义是前进，而非驻足，但您却将时间倾注在一众过客身上，并认为这是…理所应当？
t_en: To Trailblaze means to forge ahead, yet you stop and spend precious time on people just passing by. All the while you believe this is... the natural course of action?

t_ch: 我从不这么认为，「钟表匠」也做出了相同的选择。
t_en: That's not what I think at all. Besides, the Watchmaker also made a similar choice.

t_ch: 但他也因此和列车告别，一生都再没有走向群星。
t_en: And because of that, he bid farewell to the Express and never walked among the stars again in his lifetime.

t_ch: 「不是所有旅途都要通向星辰大海」，就算离开了列车，有些「开拓」也不会结束。
t_en: "Not all journeys lead to the stars." Even if we leave the Express, some Trailblazing paths will still continue.

t_ch: 你可以这么认为，无名客从来不是一个人的称呼。对于这条道路，每个人也有不同的看法，只要条件允许，我们都会尽己所能。
t_en: You can think of it this way: The title of Nameless doesn't belong to any one person. Each individual will have their own view of what it means to walk this path, and will always try their best, no matter the situation.

t_ch: 而我开拓的对象并非未知的世界，而是这片星空下，每一个活生生的人。
t_en: The goal of my Trailblazing is not the unknown world, but every living being who exists under the stars.

t_ch: 「瓦尔特」这个名字，在我的故乡代表「世界」。它喻示每个人都自成天地，其可能性和远方的群星无异。与人们产生的联结，才是我在旅途中最宝贵的收获。
t_en: In my homeland, the name "Welt" means "world." It suggests that each person is a world unto themselves, and that we harbor as many possibilities as the distant stars. The connections I gain on my journey are what is most precious to me.

t_ch: 这就是我的「开拓」之道，也是我停留于此的原因。
t_en: This is my way of the Trailblaze and the reason why I decided to stay here.

t_ch: 为何是我？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 我说过自己离开故乡的时候同样匆忙，能理解你如今的心情。
t_en: Like I said before, I left my home in a hurry, too. I know how you feel right now.

t_ch: 我也深知，只有当一个人决意告别过去的时候，他才会坦然面对全部的自我。就像此时此刻，你所做的一样……
t_en: I also know that a person will only be able to truly face themselves once they've decided to say goodbye to their past... the same way you're doing right here, right now...

t_ch: 你我身边，何曾有过第三个人？
t_en: There was never a third person with us, was there?

t_ch: 我就说会被发现的吧？
t_en: I told you he'd find out.

t_ch: 果然什么都瞒不过您的慧眼。
t_en: I should have known nothing could escape your keen eyes.

t_ch: 既然您已看穿万维克的秘密，我也无需铺垫了。走吧——
t_en: Since you already know Wonweek's secret, there's no need for me to build up suspense.

t_ch: 我们直奔主题，为这场告别画上最后的句点。
t_en: Let's go. We'll head straight for the finale and take our final bow in this farewell performance.

t_ch: 我还资历尚浅，只能看运气了。
t_en: I'm still inexperienced, so let's just hope we get lucky.

t_ch: 麻烦您了。第一块拼图，我希望它是星空的一部分。
t_en: Thank you anyway. I expect the first jigsaw piece will be a part of the starry sky.

t_ch: 而另一块，我希望它能象征羽翼。
t_en: As for the other piece, I hope it will symbolize wings.

t_ch: 足够了，我们回去吧。
t_en: We have enough. Let's go back.

t_ch: 就算你能隐藏面貌，擅闯家族禁地也未免大胆了些。
t_en: I know you can hide your appearance, but trespassing on restricted Family grounds is still a bold move.

t_ch: 放心，不会花太多时间的。
t_en: Don't worry. I won't take too much time.

t_ch: 我第一次来大剧院时还是个孩子。从这里望去，我被幕布后边的光芒吸引，深信那就是指引梦想之地的启明。
t_en: I was a child when I first visited the Grand Theater. I remember seeing the light behind the curtains from here and believing it to be the Morning Star guiding me toward the Land of the Dreams.

t_ch: 但多年以后，当我成为橡木家主再临此地，才知道那道光芒的背后是「星核」。
t_en: When I returned here many years later as the head of the Oak Family, I finally learned that the light was actually a Stellaron.

t_ch: 这次重返舞台，你准备做些什么？
t_en: Now that you've returned to the stage, what are you planning to do?

t_ch: 为了启程。我向来认为，一个人的起点应当是他的终点。
t_en: Prepare for my departure. I've always believed that a person's starting point should also be his last stop.

t_ch: 您是否见过寿终正寝的老人？当生命迎来终结，他们往往会抬起双手，尽力伸向虚空，恰如婴儿呱呱坠地，最初的姿态也是高举双臂。
t_en: Have you ever seen the elderly at the end of their lives? As they welcome their final moments, they invariably raise their hands, stretching their arms into the emptiness. Much like how babies also stretch their arms into the air when they are first born.

t_ch: 于诞生之时，于归去之刻，人类两度振翅，行于旅途始末。为了能够再次振翅飞翔……
t_en: Humanity flaps their wings twice as they complete their journey: Once when they are born, and once when they die. In order to flap my own wings and soar again...

t_ch: 我将在这里，告别「昨日的自己」。
t_en: I will say goodbye here to the me of yesterday.

t_ch: 唉，真讨厌这副面孔啊，感觉表情都生动不起来。
t_en: I really hate this face. My expressions feel so dull and lifeless.

t_ch: 原来如此，这就是你的仪容始终有些微妙的原因。
t_en: I see. That explains why something always felt off about your appearance.

t_ch: 自我们重逢起，那象征「同谐」的天环就从未在你头顶显现。
t_en: The halo above your head that symbolizes the Harmony — it's been missing since the moment we reunited.

t_ch: 诚然，那圆环是种群的天赐，但它未必不可毁弃。
t_en: It's true that this ring was bestowed upon our people by the heavens, but that doesn't mean it can't be thrown away.

t_ch: 在入梦的起点，那条思绪的长廊上，我选择将自身的天环彻底剥离。
t_en: While I was in the Gallery of Thoughts, at the entrance of the dream, I chose to completely sever the halo from myself.

t_ch: 为什么要这么做？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 我毕竟是一介逃犯，理应断绝所有被家族感知的可能。
t_en: I am a fugitive, after all. I must remove any chance of being detected by The Family.

t_ch: 另一个原因，则是为了苦痛…如同折翼坠地般，必能令我清醒的疼痛。
t_en: Another reason is to feel pain... A pain like falling from the sky with broken wings that can keep me awake.

t_ch: 如此一来，我才能将「同谐」与「秩序」的祝福尽数舍弃，生平初次，以凡人的身份踏入这美梦之中。
t_en: This is how I can cast aside all the blessings of the Harmony and the Order and, for the first time in my life, enter the sweet dream as an ordinary person.

t_ch: 所以万维克先生也是因此而生？
t_en: Is that also how Mr. Wonweek came into this world?

t_ch: 哼，他说出来你可能都不信。
t_en: Hmph, no, you wouldn't believe the real story even if he told you.

t_ch: 他的诞生源自一场意外，而且您无比熟悉……
t_en: He was born from an accident, one that you're actually quite familiar with...

t_ch: 说来有些尴尬，是那「不要笑挑战」。
t_en: It was that try-not-to-laugh challenge. Quite embarrassing, to be honest.

t_ch: 我以普通人的姿态四处漫游，不幸落入了皮皮西人的陷阱。那道令停云女士四分五裂的恶作剧，也同样将虚弱的我一分为二。
t_en: I was roaming around as an ordinary person when I unfortunately fell into that Pepeshi's trap. The prank that fragmented Miss Tingyun also divided my fragile self into two.

t_ch: 你的意思是，他是你的一部分？这倒是出人意料，你们看起来截然不同。
t_en: Are you saying that he's a part of you? That's quite surprising, given that you two looked nothing alike.

t_ch: 又或者，是另一种可能吧。
t_en: You could say he's another possibility of me.

t_ch: 我也曾是个孩子，有过深埋在心底的种种念想。那些童年的声音伴随我长大，逐渐被时光磨平，几不可闻。
t_en: I, too, was once a child, with aspirations buried deep within. Over time, those childhood voices grew increasingly faint.

t_ch: 也许儿时的我做出一个微小的改变，就会变得与他别无二致。
t_en: Maybe if I had made a small change in my childhood, I would have turned out to be exactly like him.

t_ch: 那你是挺倒霉的，没能成为更好的自己。
t_en: What a pity, failing to become a better version of yourself.

t_ch: 所以，我的最后一场告别，也和那位停云女士一样：我会对自身进行调律，重归于完整的自我。
t_en: So, my final farewell will be like that of Miss Tingyun: I will perform a tuning on myself and return to my complete form.

t_ch: 而这也意味着万维克会彻底消失。所以我才说，这是最后一站。
t_en: And this also means that Wonweek will vanish for good. That's why I said this was the last stop.

t_ch: 有没有一种可能，消失的会是你？
t_en: Is there a possibility that you will be the one who disappears?

t_ch: 没有可能，对此我们早有定论。
t_en: No, this is a foregone conclusion.

t_ch: 但事到如今，谁又能保证绝不会出现变数呢？也许内心深处，我也期待着这种可能吧。
t_en: But who's to say there won't be any changes? Maybe deep down, I'm also anticipating this possibility.

t_ch: 无论结果如何，会有人替你见证。
t_en: Regardless of the outcome, you will have a witness.

t_ch: 我在观众席上等你。
t_en: I'll be waiting for you in the audience.

t_ch: 感激不尽。
t_en: Much appreciated.

t_ch: 那我们开始吧？
t_en: Now, shall we begin?

t_ch: 「期待这种可能」…你的确是变了。换作从前，你根本不可能忍受得了杂音。
t_en: "Anticipating this possibility..." You've really changed. In the past, you wouldn't have tolerated any noise at all.

t_ch: 我毕竟要踏上一段未知的旅程，不可能再维持井然有序的生活。就像今天这样，我将不得不和意外同行。
t_en: I'm about to embark on an unknown journey, where it will be impossible to maintain my orderly life. I will have to navigate unexpected surprises, much like today.

t_ch: 其实你压根没做好准备，对吧？
t_en: You're not ready at all, are you?

t_ch: 是的。我感到不安，甚至惶恐。离开匹诺康尼后，我不再能控制一切。
t_en: I feel unsettled, maybe even scared. Ever since I left Penacony, I could no longer control everything.

t_ch: 你想多了，是一切都不能控制。
t_en: Don't flatter yourself. You can't control anything.

t_ch: 可大多数人不都是这么过日子的？
t_en: But isn't that how it is for most people?

t_ch: 对未知过分恐惧，也许就是我的不足。但你的出现证明了意外不全是坏事。
t_en: My fear of the unknown may be a weakness, but your presence has shown me that surprises aren't always bad.

t_ch: 与你相遇令我受益良多，我很高兴能以这种形式邂逅自己的另一面，也很庆幸能有机会亲口对你说出……
t_en: Meeting you has been a great help to me, and I'm glad to have discovered this side of myself. I also feel grateful for the opportunity to tell you this in person...

t_ch: 我果然很讨厌你。
t_en: I truly dislike you.

t_ch: 什么情况，你确定没说反？
t_en: What? Are you sure you didn't mean the opposite?

t_ch: 你拥有的诸多品性，确实潜藏在我心中。在许多场合，我也想说上几句俏皮话，直截了当地表达心中所想，或是毫无顾虑地展露对一个人的厌恶……
t_en: I must admit, many of your qualities strike a chord with me. There are times when I want to joke around, freely express my thoughts, or openly share my dislike of a person...

t_ch: 或者喜爱。我也想告诉她，我喜欢她的每一首歌。
t_en: Or my fondness. I want to tell her that I love all her songs.

t_ch: 那你就去做啊，老日。你总说要把小鸟关在笼子里，结果你自己才是被囚禁的那个。
t_en: Then go for it, Sunny. You always talk about locking the bird in the cage, but you're the one who's trapped.

t_ch: 照照镜子吧，自律、纠结——你的生活除了这俩还剩什么？活得像块大理石，摔下来的时候也只能粉粉碎。
t_en: Look at yourself. Besides discipline and hesitation, there's nothing else in your life. If you continue living like a slab of marble, you'll break into pieces when you fall.

t_ch: 但我不会成为你。我做不到。
t_en: But I won't become like you. I simply can't.

t_ch: 我讨厌轻佻的人，就连模仿他们的样子也会感到不适。即便经历失败，我也不会轻易放下某些坚持，比如「得体」，比如「高洁」。
t_en: I dislike people who are frivolous. It makes me uncomfortable to even emulate them. If I fail, I will still uphold my principles, such as decorum and stateliness.

t_ch: 而对于「同谐」，我的态度大体相同。你也好，命途也罢，如果我曾经质疑乃至抗拒的事物，偏偏是我今后要倚仗的力量……
t_en: As for the Harmony, my stance remains the same. Whether it's you or the Paths, if what I once doubted — or even opposed — becomes the force I must rely on in the future...

t_ch: 那么这一次，我会将「调律」交予本心。
t_en: Then this time, my true heart will guide the tuning.

t_ch: 我会请你来完成。
t_en: I'll leave it to you.

t_ch: 这算什么？朝天上丢出一枚硬币，然后把命交给它来决定？
t_en: What is this? Leaving your fate entirely up to a coin flip?

t_ch: 你根本不是这种人，脑子被列车撞傻了？还是被公司的赌徒夺舍了？
t_en: This isn't like you at all. Did you lose your mind after being run over by the Express, or were you possessed by that IPC gambler?

t_ch: 失败总是最好的老师。要拯救更多的人，你必须先理解他们是为何而生，为何而死，最好的方法就是亲身践行。
t_en: Failure is always the best teacher. To save more lives, you must first understand what they live for and what they die for. The best way to achieve this is through personal experience.

t_ch: 你看，我已经在试着做出改变了。
t_en: You see? I'm already starting to make changes.

t_ch: 合二为一也好，一方消失也罢，我将这场调律的结果交由命运，并以此验明——我能否敞开怀抱，接纳一切自己厌憎的事物。
t_en: Whether it's us merging or one of us disappearing, I'll leave the tuning to fate, and see if I can embrace all that I detest.

t_ch: 唯有如此，我才能真正迈向人世，与众生一同行走大地。
t_en: Only then will I truly be able to walk among the living in their world.

t_ch: 那如果你做不到呢？
t_en: But what if you can't do it?

t_ch: 那也无可奈何，说明我和过去一样……
t_en: Well, that's out of my hands. It will just prove that I'm still the same person...

t_ch: 不过是个只会让妹妹失望的人。
t_en: The person who always disappoints his sister...

t_ch: 直至最后，我将和弱者一同…将双手伸向天际。
t_en: And with my last breath, I'll join the weak... in reaching my hands toward the sky.

t_ch: 在这盛会之星，无论我有过怎样的身份，它们都已不再重要。
t_en: On the Planet of Festivities, whatever identities I once had, they no longer matter.

t_ch: 回应我们的不会是神明，而是属于明天的自己。
t_en: It's not the Gods who answer us, but ourselves from the future.

t_ch: 我似乎也没什么好说的。但既然要说再见了，给你最后一个建议吧。
t_en: I don't have much to say. But as it's time to part ways, here's one last piece of advice.

t_ch: 讲俏皮话要用心，别用技巧——更不要每次都解释。
t_en: When telling jokes, be sincere. Don't sweat the details, and above all, don't explain them every time.

t_ch: 不过算了，对你而言，还是别讲更好。
t_en: Ah, never mind. Maybe it's better for you not to joke at all.

t_ch: 我无法否认。
t_en: No objections from me.

t_ch: 就是这点最让人恼火，我怎么会变成这么无聊的人啊。
t_en: This is so annoying. How did I become so dull?

t_ch: 但就算是这样，也别让自己太辛苦啊。总有人会伤心难过的。
t_en: Well, despite everything, try not to be too hard on yourself. There will always be people who are sad.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Mm.

t_ch: 现在，我该怎么称呼你？星期日先生，还是万维克先生？
t_en: Well, how should I address you now? Mr. Sunday or Mr. Wonweek?

t_ch: 按照您的意愿就好。也许，他正在我心间冷嘲热讽吧。
t_en: Whichever you prefer. He's probably mocking me from inside right now.

t_ch: 感谢您一路同行，瓦尔特先生。这段告别之旅到此为止了。
t_en: Thank you for accompanying me through all this, Mr. Yang. This farewell journey has come to an end.

t_ch: 离开匹诺康尼后，你有什么打算？
t_en: What are your plans after leaving Penacony?

t_ch: 事到如今，也无需再向您隐瞒。我认为匹诺康尼星核一事，背后另有隐情。
t_en: At this point, there is no need to hide things from you. I believe there's more to the incident involving the Stellaron on Penacony.

t_ch: 你的意思是，操纵星核的人并非梦主？
t_en: Are you suggesting that the Dreammaster wasn't the one controlling the Stellaron?

t_ch: 请勿担心。篡夺星核，假借谐乐大典复活已死的星神，这场动乱的主谋确实是橡木家系，各位已经战胜了幕后黑手。
t_en: Don't worry. The Oak Family was indeed behind seizing the Stellaron and resurrecting the fallen Aeon during the Charmony Festival, and you've already defeated them.

t_ch: 但歌斐木先生曾对我说，如今「秩序」所做的一切，都是为了不再重蹈「同谐」的覆辙……
t_en: But Mr. Gopher Wood once told me that all the Order's efforts were aimed at avoiding the same mistakes that the Harmony made.
